raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"Section is structured around a contribution claim (38% p99 reduction, queue-aware admission) and closes with an explicit novelty summary; reconstructing and verifying that thesis framing is central to any meaningful review.","R1.2":"A multi-step causal chain (queue-depth estimate → admission decision → tail-latency improvement) must be internally coherent; a subject-verb tense mismatch ('compared' vs. 'compares') and several comma-spliced run-on sentences signal presentation-logic gaps that a review should flag.","R1.3":"The 38% p99 figure is the section's headline claim and is load-bearing for credibility; it forward-references §4 with no inline qualification, so verifying that the reference is sufficient and no intermediate claims exceed what the cited evaluation supports is squarely in scope.","R1.4":"The closing sentence asserts first-of-kind status ('first multi-LLM routing system … that we are aware of'); assessing whether this novelty claim is defensible against nearest prior work is directly triggered by the artifact.","R1.6":"Multiple run-on sentences (notably the 70-word queue-maintenance sentence and the ring-buffer sentence), a tense error, and comma splices are visible; prose quality is reviewable under any plausible interpretation of the delegation."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize writing and editorial quality (grammar, sentence clarity, narrative flow), or technical and content correctness (logical soundness, evidence-claim alignment, novelty validity)?"}

rationale.R1.1: Section is structured around a contribution claim (38% p99 reduction, queue-aware admission) and closes with an explicit novelty summary; reconstructing and verifying that thesis framing is central to any meaningful review.
rationale.R1.2: A multi-step causal chain (queue-depth estimate → admission decision → tail-latency improvement) must be internally coherent; a subject-verb tense mismatch ('compared' vs. 'compares') and several comma-spliced run-on sentences signal presentation-logic gaps that a review should flag.
rationale.R1.3: The 38% p99 figure is the section's headline claim and is load-bearing for credibility; it forward-references §4 with no inline qualification, so verifying that the reference is sufficient and no intermediate claims exceed what the cited evaluation supports is squarely in scope.
rationale.R1.4: The closing sentence asserts first-of-kind status ('first multi-LLM routing system … that we are aware of'); assessing whether this novelty claim is defensible against nearest prior work is directly triggered by the artifact.
rationale.R1.6: Multiple run-on sentences (notably the 70-word queue-maintenance sentence and the ring-buffer sentence), a tense error, and comma splices are visible; prose quality is reviewable under any plausible interpretation of the delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize writing and editorial quality (grammar, sentence clarity, narrative flow), or technical and content correctness (logical soundness, evidence-claim alignment, novelty validity)?
